description_long: This is a double-faced sledgehammer with a robust, cylindrical metal head and a long, straight handle. The head is symmetrical, featuring two identical flat striking faces, each with slightly beveled edges, designed for delivering powerful blows. The handle appears to be made of a dark, possibly composite or reinforced material, and is straight with a consistent thickness, providing a secure grip for two-handed use. The metal head has a matte, slightly worn finish, indicating it is built for heavy-duty tasks such as breaking concrete or driving stakes. There are no visible markings, logos, or decorative elements on either the head or the handle. The overall design is utilitarian, prioritizing function and durability for use in construction, demolition, or urban maintenance settings.
description: A heavy-duty double-faced sledgehammer with a cylindrical metal head and a long, straight dark handle, built for powerful impact tasks.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the double-faced cylindrical metal head and the straight handle extending downward.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the narrow profile of the hammer head and the straight, dark handle.
description_view_2: Rear view, identical to the front, again showing the full width of the double-faced head and the handle.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring view 1, with the narrow profile of the head and handle visible.
attribute_handle_type: Straight composite handle, dark in color, with a smooth and consistent cylindrical shape, likely designed for a secure two-handed grip.
attribute_head_type: Double-faced sledgehammer head, cylindrical in shape, made of steel with two identical flat striking faces and slightly beveled edges.
